title: Mechanical rename: type Foo {} -> struct Foo {} throughout spec
description: The design decision in §1.4 of ferrum-lang-types.md chose 'struct' for product types and 'type' for aliases/constraints. The spec examples have not been updated. This is a mechanical search-replace across all .md files: 'type <Name> {' -> 'struct <Name> {' and 'type <Name>[<params>] {' -> 'struct <Name>[<params>] {'. Approximately 396 occurrences across ~30 files. Care needed to not change type aliases (type X = ...) or constrained types (type X = ... where ...).
close_reason: Complete. 61 files, ~814 occurrences renamed. Type aliases and constrained types untouched.
